title: Netanyahu Discloses He Was Treated for Prostate Cancer, Raising Health Transparency Questions
detail: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu publicly disclosed that he was successfully treated for prostate cancer, prompting questions about health transparency and political succession. The announcement drew scrutiny from opposition leaders and analysts who questioned why the diagnosis and treatment had not been revealed sooner, with implications for governance continuity during the ongoing Gaza conflict.

media.caption: Netanyahu discloses prostate cancer treatment, April 24, 2026

title: IDF Strikes Kill 12 Palestinians in Deadliest Single-Day Gaza Attack in Weeks — Police Vehicle in Khan Younis, Beit Lahiya, Sheikh Radwan
detail: Israeli forces conducted multiple strikes across Gaza on April 24, 2026, killing at least 11–12 Palestinians in the deadliest single-day attack in weeks. Key strikes: (1) An Israeli airstrike struck a Hamas police patrol vehicle in Khan Younis, killing at least 7–8 people including civilian bystanders — among the worst single-incident death tolls since the October 2025 ceasefire; (2) Two Hamas police officers were killed in a strike on a patrol near the Sheikh Radwan Police Station in northwestern Gaza City; (3) Two people were killed when Israeli forces bombed a house in Beit Lahiya in northern Gaza. The attacks came after Hamas demanded full implementation of Phase 1 ceasefire terms, including Israeli military withdrawal. Gaza's Ministry of Health confirmed the deaths. The IDF said strikes targeted Hamas-affiliated armed operatives. Antiwar.com noted a 46% increase in Israeli attacks over the prior week per UN tracking.

media.caption: Israeli strikes kill at least 12 Palestinians in Gaza in deadliest single-day attack in weeks, April 24, 2026

title: IDF Artillery Kills Pregnant Palestinian Woman and Two Children Near Kamal Adwan Hospital, Beit Lahia
detail: Israeli artillery struck residential homes near Kamal Adwan Hospital in Beit Lahia, northern Gaza, on April 24, 2026, killing Islam Al-Tanani — who was pregnant with twins — along with her young son Hamza and infant daughter Naya. Her husband Khalid Al-Tanani survived and recounted: 'I went inside and found my wife, Islam Al-Tanani, martyred, and my son, Hamza, and Naya in her mother's arms.' The Al-Tanani family's funerals were held on April 25. The strike near the only functioning hospital in northern Gaza drew international condemnation. Gaza's civil defense agency confirmed the deaths. The incident contributed to the April 24 death toll of at least 11–12 Palestinians. Since the October 2025 ceasefire, Palestinian health authorities have documented 811 deaths and 2,278 injuries as of April 26.

media.caption: Pregnant Palestinian woman Islam Al-Tanani and two children killed by Israeli artillery near Kamal Adwan Hospital, Beit Lahia, April 24, 2026
